# fluid-tailwindcss
> Tailwind CSS plugin for fluid responsive utilities using CSS clamp(), with Tailwind CSS v4 support and tailwind-merge integration.

## Core Files

- `src/index.ts`: Main plugin entry point, option normalization, Tailwind `matchUtilities` registration, public exports.
- `src/clamp.ts`: CSS `clamp()` calculation, fluid parsing, validation integration, accessibility checks.
- `src/length.ts`: CSS length parsing and rem/px conversion.
- `src/utilities.ts`: Source of truth for supported `fl-*` utility names and CSS property mappings.
- `src/validation.ts`: Unit matching, breakpoint/value validation, arbitrary value helpers.
- `src/errors.ts`: Typed error codes and `FluidError` helpers.
- `src/types.ts`: Public and internal TypeScript types.
- `src/tailwind-merge/index.ts`: Fluid class validators and `tailwind-merge` class group configuration.
- `tests/`: Vitest tests covering plugin behavior, clamp math, validation, merge behavior, and performance.
- `homepage/`: Vite React documentation/demo site using the local package.

## Key Concepts

- Fluid utility syntax: `fl-{utility}-{min}/{max}`.
- Example: `fl-p-4/8` emits padding that scales from Tailwind spacing `4` to `8`.
- Tailwind v4 sends slash syntax through `extra.modifier`; handlers reconstruct `value/modifier`.
- Negative plugin utilities use `neg-fl-*` instead of `-fl-*`.
- Clamp math converts supported units to rem internally, then emits rem or px depending on options.
- `generateFluidValues()` intentionally registers O(n) values, not O(n²) min/max pairs.
- `src/utilities.ts` and `src/tailwind-merge/index.ts` must stay synchronized.

## Main Commands

```bash
pnpm test:run
pnpm lint
pnpm build
```

Homepage:

```bash
cd homepage
pnpm dev
pnpm build
pnpm lint
```

## Do Not

- Do not edit `dist/` manually.
- Do not remove lowercase option aliases without treating it as a breaking compatibility change.
- Do not add a new `fl-*` utility without updating merge behavior and tests.
- Do not reintroduce O(n²) utility pair generation.
